issues: The draft describes the Toronto vs Calgary game recap, but the Sockeyes-relevant content is the Florida Panthers section, which lists ex-Sockeye Dave Tomlinson (#17) as a healthy scratch for the Panthers.

The draft's long description incorrectly states 'two additional goals by Andreychuk and Wood for Toronto' — Andreychuk scored goals 1 and 3 for Toronto (not two additional), and the game-winning goal was his second of the night (goal #5 overall), not 'in the third period for his second goal' as the only framing.
The draft entirely omits the Sockeyes-relevant Florida Panthers roster/notes section, which is the primary reason this clipping is in the archive.
Dave Tomlinson (#17, C) is listed in both the Panthers 21-game totals roster and as a healthy scratch in the game notes — this is the archive-relevant content.
review.correctedHeadline: Maple Leafs 3, Flames 2 — Andreychuk Gets Winner
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Florida Panthers game notes and 21-game roster totals listing ex-Richmond Sockeye Dave Tomlinson (#17, C) as a healthy scratch.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A Florida Panthers game notes column in The Miami Herald lists ex-Richmond Sockeyes centre Dave Tomlinson (#17) among the healthy scratches alongside Joe Cirella and Andrei Lomakin. The page also includes a 21-game Panthers roster table in which Tomlinson appears at #17, listed at 5-11, 190 lbs. The page additionally contains the box score and brief recap of the Toronto Maple Leafs' 3-2 win over the Calgary Flames, in which Dave Andreychuk scored twice including the power-play winner in the third period.
review.notes: The AI draft focused on the Maple Leafs/Flames recap (adjacent-column content) and missed the Sockeyes-relevant Florida Panthers section, which is the reason this clipping was archived. Dave Tomlinson, ex-1987 Richmond Sockeyes, is explicitly named as a healthy scratch and appears in the Panthers roster table. The Viewer URL also confirms the search terms were "Dave Tomlinson" and "Maple Leafs," suggesting the clipping was found via Tomlinson's name.
